what: block_m=64 for bs=128/E=33/d=512 with 4-WG M128 stage1 + FlyDSL t16x128x128 stage2 (instead of block_m=128)
why: block_m controls moe_sorting granularity. With 128 tokens and 33 experts (~3.9 tokens/expert avg), block_m=128 means most expert groups fit in 1 block with heavy padding. block_m=64 halves the padding waste while still having reasonable tile count for CU utilization. The 4-WG stage1 kernel (M128) handles the compute tiles independently.

what: block_m=64 for bs=512/E=33/d=512 and d=2048 with 4-WG M128 stage1 + FlyDSL stage2 (instead of block_m=128)
why: block_m=64 just improved bs=128/E=33 by -1.4%. With 512 tokens and 33 experts (~15.5 tokens/expert avg), block_m=128 means most expert groups only need 1 block with ~50% padding waste. block_m=64 provides 2 blocks per typical expert with less padding waste, better CU distribution.

what: block_m=16 for bs=512/E=257/d=256 with 4-WG M32 stage1 + FlyDSL stage2 (was block_m=32)
why: block_m=64 improved E=33/bs=512 shapes significantly. For E=257/bs=512, tokens per expert is ~2 on average (512*9/257=18, but with load imbalance many experts get 0-2 tokens). block_m=16 matches the sparse distribution better than block_m=32, reducing padding waste.

what: block_m=64 for bs=512/E=257/d=256 with 4-WG M32 stage1 + FlyDSL stage2 (was block_m=32)
why: block_m=64 significantly improved E=33/bs=512 shapes (-3.2%). For E=257/bs=512, avg ~18 tokens/expert. block_m=64 gives better CU distribution than block_m=32 which creates too many small tiles. v161 showed block_m=16 crashes due to quant assert.
override: v161 tried block_m=16 which crashed, this tries block_m=64 in the opposite direction
